codeReview.summary: The commit adds a cipher runtime and unit tests, but it does not integrate encryption/decryption into the actual relay send/read path. Relay transport and server handling still use plaintext `body`, so the feature does not satisfy ciphertext-only wire delivery or recipient decrypt-on-receive behavior.

issues.description: Outbound relay requests still send plaintext `body` (`body: payload.body`) instead of ciphertext fields. This violates expected behavior 'Relay/wire payloads never include plaintext message body' (VAL-E2EE-003).

Server `/messages` handler still reads plaintext `body` and stores/returns it (`messageBody = body['body']`, then `body: messageBody`). No decrypt path is wired for recipients, and `encryptMessage`/`decryptMessage` are not used in relay code (only in cipher module/tests), so VAL-E2EE-004 and true relay-path VAL-E2EE-009 coverage are not met.

summary: Review failed: feature implementation is limited to crypto primitives and unit tests, without relay path integration. Plaintext message body is still transmitted and handled on relay endpoints, so ciphertext-only delivery/decrypt behavior is not implemented end-to-end.
